title: LAP: Language-Action Pre-Training Enables Zero-shot Cross-Embodiment Transfer
summary: LAP is a vision-language-action model that achieves zero-shot generalization to unseen robot embodiments by representing low-level actions in natural language during pre-training, unifying action learning with visual question answering under a single language-modeling objective. By supervising the VLA backbone with natural-language action descriptions, LAP bridges the gap between action semantics and language representations, enabling cross-embodiment transfer without target-embodiment demonstrations. LAP-3B achieves over 50% success rate on unseen embodiments zero-shot and requires approximately 2.5x fewer demonstrations for fine-tuning than comparable baselines.
